conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out! To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child, Alex, who's 17.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 17, I need to ask: Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in college doing A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful, thank you! Now I need to ask: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK, in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Now, does Alex live with you, or do they live somewhere else (for example, with someone else or in residential accommodation)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to complete the assessment. 

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you! One final question: Is Alex working 24 hours or more per week, or are they receiving any benefits in their own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work that much - just focuses on college. And no, they don't get any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- You live in the UK and are responsible for Alex, who lives with you
- Although Alex is 17 (over 16), you still qualify because Alex is under 20 and in approved education (doing A-levels at college)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in their own right

You can proceed with your Child Benefit claim. Just remember that if Alex's circumstances change - such as finishing college, starting work for 24+ hours per week, or starting to receive certain benefits - you'll need to inform HMRC as this may affect your entitlement.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at college). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives at home with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit), which would otherwise stop Child Benefit. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.
